# DEEP SYNTHESIS REPORT: Phases 13, 14, 15 — Session Trackers, Monitor/Notification, and Delegate-Task Gap Remediation

**Synthesized:** 2026-05-22
**Files consumed:** 52 `.md` files across 3 phase directories

---

## 1. Phase-by-Phase Summary Table

| Dimension | Phase 13 | Phase 14 | Phase 15 |
|-----------|----------|----------|----------|
| **Title** | Session Tracker Defect Remediation | Wire Monitor/Notification into DelegationManager | Delegate-Task Gap Remediation (Resume, Delivery, Rich Notifications) |
| **Domain** | Session capture pipeline — hooks → disk | Delegation lifecycle monitoring, failure detection, TUI notifications | Surgical gap fixes for resume, pending replay, rich notifications, control actions, completion detection |
| **Starting State** | Pipeline BROKEN — 4/5 child sessions dual-registered; turnCount always 0; child .json files are skeletons (1 turn); legacy event-tracker double-capturing | Partial edits exist but unverified; escalation-timer design fundamentally wrong (WARN→NUDGE→ALERT→TERMINATE); 65% spec compliance | Phase 14 implemented ~65% spec — 3 critical gaps (C1-C3), 3 medium gaps (M1-M3), 2 minor gaps (N1-N2) |
| **Sub-plans** | 6 (01-06) | 4 (01-04) | 5 (01-05) |
| **Total sub-tasks** | ~18 (2-4 per plan) | ~10 (2-3 per plan) | ~14 (2-3 per plan) |
| **Files modified** | ~16 source + test files | ~15 source + test + artifacts | ~12 source + test files |
| **Tests** | 256 session-tracker tests; 12 integration tests | 271 delegation tests across 11 files; 2384 full-suite tests | 259-282 delegation tests across 15 files |
| **REQs covered** | 13 (REQ-ST-01 through 13) | 8 (P14-R1 through R8) | 6 (REQ-01 through 06) + GAP-N1 |
| **Completion** | ✅ Complete — all 13 REQs | ✅ Complete — all 8 REQs (live UAT still BLOCKED) | ✅ Complete — all 6 REQs, all 8 gaps closed |
| **Methodology** | Disk-truth-first (L1 evidence before any claim) | TDD RED→GREEN; dirty-worktree snapshots; semantic cleanup audit | TDD RED→GREEN; gap-analysis-driven; pure function additions |
| **Architecture impact** | Repaired existing session-tracker pipeline; single-path init; serial write queues | New dispatch path for monitor/notification wiring; control tools suite; slot management | sendPromptAsync injection path; 4-condition completion; pending replay; rich notifications |

---

## 2. CROSS-CUTTING PATTERNS

### 2.1 Methodology Continuity
All three phases share a **TDD RED→GREEN→REFACTOR** pattern with atomic commits per sub-task. Each plan file includes `<must_haves>` with typed truths, `<behavior>` with test specs, and automated verification commands. Phase 13 uniquely uses **disk-truth-first** (L1 evidence validation against `.hivemind/` file content) — this corrected a prior false claim that "12 of 14 defects fixed in code."

### 2.2 Dependency Injection at Composition Root
All three phases reinforce the pattern established earlier: `src/plugin.ts` is the **sole composition root**. Dependencies are constructed in `setupDelegationModules()` and passed as constructor options with `Pick<Interface, "methodName">` typed partials. No singleton globals, no module-level state.

### 2.3 Serial Write Queues
Phase 13 established the `enqueueWrite` + `detectStaleQueue` pattern for concurrent filesystem safety. This was applied to `SessionIndexWriter` (per-session queues) and `ChildWriter` (per-child-file queues). Phase 14 and 15 extend this pattern into the delegation layer but don't need filesystem-level queues (they use in-memory state via lifecycle + continuity).

### 2.4 Hook-Observer Boundary
The CQRS rule (hooks observe, manager/coordinator mutates) is respected across all three phases:
- **Phase 13:** Hooks → `sessionTracker.handleX()` methods → persistence layer (no direct fs writes from hooks)
- **Phase 14:** `chat.message` → `delegationManager.recordChildMessageSignal()`; `tool.execute.after` → `recordChildToolSignal()`
- **Phase 15:** No new hook patterns — all changes are in coordination/manager/plugin layers

### 2.5 WaiterModel Dispatch Preservation
All phases preserve the existing WaiterModel dispatch pattern (async delegation via `sendPromptAsync` → child session runs independently → monitor polls for completion). Phase 15 specifically adds a *second* dispatch path for resume/chain (`sendPromptAsync` to existing child session) that explicitly preserves the WaiterModel — it does NOT create a new session.

---

## 3. NOTIFICATION DELIVERY MECHANISMS — COMPLETE MAP

### 3.1 All Pathways

| # | Mechanism | SDK Method(s) | Where Wired | Used For | Phase |
|---|-----------|---------------|-------------|----------|-------|
| **N1** | TUI append (thin-line) | `client.tui.appendPrompt({ text })` | `plugin.ts` deliver callback; wrapped as `appendTuiPrompt(client, text)` | Live parent session notification — progress, success, failure | 14 |
| **N2** | TUI toast | `client.tui.showToast({ message, variant })` | `plugin.ts:164` — **REMOVED in Phase 15** | Was redundant alongside N1; Phase 15 GAP-N1 removed it | 14 (removed in 15) |
| **N3** | Session prompt (system_reminder blocks) | `client.session.prompt({ noReply: true, parts: [...] })` | `src/task-management/lifecycle/index.ts:replayPendingNotifications()` via `notification-handler.ts` | Full system_reminder delivery when parent session is known to be alive | 14, 15 |
| **N4** | Pending notification queue → continuity | `patchSessionContinuity(sessionID, { pendingNotifications: [...] })` | `plugin.ts:persistPendingDelegationNotifications()` | When parent session ended → notification queued for later replay | 14, 15 |
| **N5** | Init-time drain from continuity | `listSessionContinuity()` → `appendTuiPrompt(client, line)` for each pending | `plugin.ts:replayPendingDelegationNotifications()` (fire-and-forget at plugin init) | Recovers notifications that were queued while session was ended | 15 |
| **N6** | Lifecycle event-triggered replay | `replayPendingNotificationsForEvent()` on `session.created`/`session.updated` | `src/task-management/lifecycle/index.ts:169-192` | Replays via N3 when parent session reconnects | 14 (pre-existing) |
| **N7** | User-direct control action | `sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, { parts: [{ type: "text", text }] })` | `DelegationManager.controlDelegation()` for resume/chain/adjust-prompt | Sends new prompt directly into an existing child session (no monitoring) | 15 |

### 3.2 Notification Delivery Flow (End-to-End)

```
Delegation completes/fails
  → Monitor.onComplete/onFailure fires
    → coordinator.handleCompletion() or manager.handleFailure()
      → notificationRouter.route(notification)
        → route() checks idempotency key (delegationId:type:message)
          → deliver callback (plugin.ts):
            (a) If session alive: N1 (appendTuiPrompt)
            (b) If session ended: N4 (queuePending → persist to continuity)
              → Later, on session resume:
                - N6 fires (lifecycle replay via N3)
                - AND/OR N5 fires (init-time drain via N1)
```

### 3.3 Critical Design Decisions

- **Idempotency:** All terminal notifications carry `idempotencyKey: ${delegationId}:${type}:${message}` to prevent duplicate delivery
- **Double-notification prevention:** Both N5 and N6 read from the same `pendingNotifications[]` array and both `patchSessionContinuity(sessionID, { pendingNotifications: [] })` after successful replay. Whichever runs first clears the array; the other sees empty.
- **`appendTuiPrompt` has NO `sessionID` parameter** in the SDK — this means multi-parent routing cannot be proven at unit-test level. Live UAT is required for L1 evidence.

### 3.4 Code Paths Where `sendPrompt()` / `session.prompt()` Are Used

| File | Line(s) | Pattern | Phase |
|------|---------|---------|-------|
| `src/shared/session-api.ts:183-195` | `sendPromptAsync(client, sessionID, body)` | Wraps `client.session.promptAsync()` | 14, 15 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager-runtime.ts:242` | `sendPromptAsync(this.client, childSessionId, promptBody)` | Initial dispatch → new child session | 14 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/sdk-child-session-starter.ts:36` | `sendPromptAsync(client, childSessionId, {...})` | SDK child session starter | 14 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~296` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Resume/chain — relivers to existing child session | 15 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~268` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Adjust-prompt — supplementary prompt to running session | 15 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~284` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Change-agent — sends prompt with new agent name | 15 |
| `src/task-management/lifecycle/index.ts:~180` | `replayPendingNotifications(client, sessionID, pending)` | Lifecycle replay via N3 | 14 (pre-existing) |
| `src/coordination/delegation/coordinator.ts:~213` | `sendPromptAsync(previousChildSessionId, step.prompt)` | Chain-append from coordinator | 15 |

---

## 4. SESSION TRACKING MECHANISMS — COMPLETE MAP

### 4.1 Session Tracker Architecture (Phase 13)

```
.hivemind/session-tracker/
├── project-continuity.json              # Index of ALL sessions
│   ├── sessions: { sessionID → { childCount, turnCount, status, ... } }
│   └── chronologicalOrder: string[]
├── {parent-session-id}/
│   ├── {parent-session-id}.md            # Main session markdown (YAML frontmatter + turns)
│   ├── session-continuity.json           # Per-session index (turnCount, children[], toolSummary)
│   ├── {child-session-1}.json            # Child session capture (turns[], status, lastMessage)
│   ├── {child-session-2}.json
│   └── ...
```

Key design points:
- **ParentID gate:** `ensureSessionReady()` queries SDK `client.session.get()` and skips directory creation for child sessions (Phase 13 F-01)
- **Single init path:** `eventCapture` owns `session.created` exclusively; `ensureSessionReady` is only for lazy bootstrap from `handleChatMessage`/`handleToolExecuteAfter`
- **Child capture:** Child `.json` files capture `turns[]` including turn 0 (delegation spawn) + all subsequent `chat.message` events via `childWriter.appendChildTurn()`
- **Turn counting:** `MessageCapture` calls `sessionIndexWriter.incrementTurnCount(sessionID)` after each user message; seeded from `.md` on restart via `seedTurnCounters()`
- **Dual indices:** `project-continuity.json` tracks `childCount`; per-session `session-continuity.json` tracks `turnCount` and `hierarchy.children`
- **Serial queues:** Per-session and per-child-file write queues with 5-minute stale detection prevent concurrent write corruption

### 4.2 Delegation Session Management (Phases 14-15)

```
DelegationManager
├── dispatch() → coordinator.dispatch() or runtime.dispatch()
│   → spawnDelegatedSession() → creates child session
│   → state.registerDelegation()
│   → notificationRouter.register(delegationId, parentSessionId)
│   → sendPromptAsync() → child runs
│   → state.transition("running")
│   → monitor.start(delegationId, parentSessionId)
│
├── controlDelegation()
│   ├── abort → markAborted
│   ├── cancel → markCancelled
│   ├── restart → abort + coordinator.dispatch (new child session)
│   ├── resume → sendPromptAsync (REUSES childSessionId) + new record with resumedFrom
│   ├── chain → sendPromptAsync (REUSES childSessionId) + new record with chainedFrom
│   ├── adjust-prompt → sendPromptAsync to running session
│   └── change-agent → abort + sendPromptAsync with new agent
│
├── recordChildMessageSignal()     ← from chat.message hook
├── recordChildToolSignal()        ← from tool.execute.after hook
└── canSessionAccessDelegation()   ← lineage-based access control
```

**Session hierarchy tracking:**
- 10 slots per parent session (enforced by `SlotManager`)
- Delegation records tracked in in-memory `Map<string, Delegation>` + persisted via `delegation-persistence.ts`
- Parent session ID stored in `Delegation.parentSessionId`; child session ID in `Delegation.childSessionId`
- `resumedFrom`, `chainedFrom`, `restartedFrom` fields on `Delegation` type trace delegation lineage
- Route notifications via `NotificationRouter.register(delegationId, parentSessionId)` — prevents cross-session pollution

---

## 5. TASK vs. DELEGATION DIFFERENTIATION

| Dimension | Native Task Tool | delegate-task Tool |
|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|
| **Mechanism** | OpenCode native subagent dispatch | SDK child-session via `sendPromptAsync` |
| **Monitoring** | None (fire-and-forget) | ✅ Progressive polling 30→45→60→90→120→180s |
| **Failure detection** | None | ✅ 60→120→180→300s action-count checkpoints |
| **Notification** | None | ✅ TUI system_reminder on completion/failure |
| **Control tools** | None | ✅ abort, cancel, restart, resume, chain, adjust-prompt, change-agent |
| **Context preservation** | None (new session each time) | ✅ Session ID tracking, resume/chain reuse same session |
| **Slot management** | None | ✅ 10 slots per parent session |
| **Schema validation** | OpenCode native | Zod schema with `.refine()` for conditional field validation |
| **PTY/Background** | N/A | N/A (covered by CP-PTY phases) |

---

## 6. SKILL / AGENT / TOOL DOCUMENTATION GENERATED

| Artifact | Phase | File | Description |
|----------|-------|------|-------------|
| DIRTY WORKTREE SNAPSHOT | 14 | `14-DIRTY-WORKTREE-SNAPSHOT.md` | Records dirty state before Phase 14 edits — protects `.hivemind/**`, `opencode.json`, and unowned planning artifacts |
| CLEANUP AUDIT | 14 | `14-DEPRECATED-DELEGATION-CLEANUP-AUDIT.md` | Semantic blocklist/allowlist for deprecated `category`/`safetyCeiling`/`classifications` cleanup |
| LIVE UAT CHECKLIST | 14 | `14-LIVE-UAT-CHECKLIST.md` | Manual runtime validation contract for TUI notification, progressive injection, failure checkpoint, parent routing |
| GAP ANALYSIS | 14 | `14-GAPS-ANALYSIS-2026-05-19.md` | Phase 14 post-hoc gap analysis — 3 critical, 3 medium, 2 minor gaps |
| PATTERNS DOCUMENTATION | 14 | `14-PATTERNS.md` | 22-file pattern map with code examples, analog assignments, and planner recommendations |
| PATTERNS DOCUMENTATION | 15 | `15-PATTERNS.md` | 6-file pattern map for gap remediation |
| SPEC LOCK | 14 | `14-SPEC.md` | 8 requirements, ambiguity score unknown |
| SPEC LOCK | 15 | `15-SPEC.md` | 6 requirements, ambiguity score 0.097 |
| DISCUSSION LOG | 15 | `15-DISCUSSION-LOG.md` | Auto-generated decision log from gap analysis |
| PLAN VERIFICATION | 15 | `15-PLAN-VERIFICATION.md` | Cross-plan dependency/contract analysis yielding 3 blockers resolved |

---

## 7. SPECIFIC CODE PATHS WHERE `sendPrompt()` / `session.prompt()` / `appendTuiPrompt()` ARE USED

### `sendPromptAsync` (wraps `client.session.promptAsync`):

| Location | Purpose | Call Pattern |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `session-api.ts:183` | Wrapper definition | `export async function sendPromptAsync(client, sessionID, body)` |
| `manager-runtime.ts:242` | Initial delegation dispatch | `await sendPromptAsync(this.client, delegation.childSessionId, promptBody)` |
| `sdk-child-session-starter.ts:36` | SDK child creation | `await sendPromptAsync(client, childSessionId, {...})` |
| `manager.ts:~296` | Resume/chain | `await this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` |
| `manager.ts:~268` | Adjust-prompt | `await this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` |
| `manager.ts:~284` | Change-agent | `await this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` (with agent note) |
| `coordinator.ts:~213` | Chain-append | `await sendPromptAsync(previousChildSessionId, step.prompt)` |

### `appendTuiPrompt` (wraps `client.tui.appendPrompt`):

| Location | Purpose | Call Pattern |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `session-api.ts:204` | Wrapper definition | `export async function appendTuiPrompt(client, text)` |
| `plugin.ts:160-162` | Deliver callback (success/failure) | `await appendTuiPrompt(options.client, line)` |
| `plugin.ts:241-249` | Init-time pending notification drain | `await appendTuiPrompt(client, line)` in `replayPendingDelegationNotifications()` |
| `notification-formatter.ts:31` | Formats `<system_reminder>` blocks | Pure function — called before appendTuiPrompt |

### `showTuiToast` (wraps `client.tui.showToast`):

| Location | Purpose | Status |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `session-api.ts:210` | Wrapper definition | ✅ Still exists |
| `plugin.ts:164` | Was "Delegation delivered" toast | ❌ **REMOVED** in Phase 15 (GAP-N1) |

---

## 8. KEY ARCHITECTURAL TRANSITIONS ACROSS PHASES

| Transition | Phase 13 | Phase 14 | Phase 15 |
|------------|----------|----------|----------|
| **Pipeline state** | Broken → Repaired | Partial → Complete | 65% → ~95%+ spec compliance |
| **Session management** | Dual indices, child.json, serial queues | In-memory delegation + continuity persistence | Resume/chain session reuse, pending replay |
| **Notification path** | None (session tracker only) | TUI append + pending queue + lifecycle replay | Init-time drain + rich fields + toast removed |
| **Control surface** | Fixes only (no new tools) | 7 control actions (abort/cancel/restart/resume/chain/adjust-prompt/change-agent) + slot management | Same 7 actions, but resume/chain now truly reuse session ID |
| **Completion** | 3 conditions (stalled + assistant + fileChanges) | Same 3 conditions | 4 conditions (+ totalToolActivityDuration > 60s) |
| **Test approach** | Disk-truth integration tests | TDD RED→GREEN with fake timers | TDD RED→GREEN with mathematical constraint validation |

| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~268` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Adjust-prompt — supplementary prompt to running session | 15 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~284` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Change-agent — sends prompt with new agent name | 15 |
| `src/task-management/lifecycle/index.ts:~180` | `replayPendingNotifications(client, sessionID, pending)` | Lifecycle replay via N3 | 14 (pre-existing) |
| `src/coordination/delegation/coordinator.ts:~213` | `sendPromptAsync(previousChildSessionId, step.prompt)` | Chain-append from coordinator | 15 |

---

## 4. SESSION TRACKING MECHANISMS — COMPLETE MAP

### 4.1 Session Tracker Architecture (Phase 13)

```
.hivemind/session-tracker/
├── project-continuity.json              # Index of ALL sessions
│   ├── sessions: { sessionID → { childCount, turnCount, status, ... } }
│   └── chronologicalOrder: string[]
├── {parent-session-id}/
│   ├── {parent-session-id}.md            # Main session markdown (YAML frontmatter + turns)
│   ├── session-continuity.json           # Per-session index (turnCount, children[], toolSummary)
│   ├── {child-session-1}.json            # Child session capture (turns[], status, lastMessage)
│   ├── {child-session-2}.json
│   └── ...
```

Key design points:
- **ParentID gate:** `ensureSessionReady()` queries SDK `client.session.get()` and skips directory creation for child sessions (Phase 13 F-01)
- **Single init path:** `eventCapture` owns `session.created` exclusively; `ensureSessionReady` is only for lazy bootstrap from `handleChatMessage`/`handleToolExecuteAfter`
- **Child capture:** Child `.json` files capture `turns[]` including turn 0 (delegation spawn) + all subsequent `chat.message` events via `childWriter.appendChildTurn()`
- **Turn counting:** `MessageCapture` calls `sessionIndexWriter.incrementTurnCount(sessionID)` after each user message; seeded from `.md` on restart via `seedTurnCounters()`
- **Dual indices:** `project-continuity.json` tracks `childCount`; per-session `session-continuity.json` tracks `turnCount` and `hierarchy.children`
- **Serial queues:** Per-session and per-child-file write queues with 5-minute stale detection prevent concurrent write corruption

### 4.2 Delegation Session Management (Phases 14-15)

```
DelegationManager
├── dispatch() → coordinator.dispatch() or runtime.dispatch()
│   → spawnDelegatedSession() → creates child session
│   → state.registerDelegation()
│   → notificationRouter.register(delegationId, parentSessionId)
│   → sendPromptAsync() → child runs
│   → state.transition("running")
│   → monitor.start(delegationId, parentSessionId)
│
├── controlDelegation()
│   ├── abort → markAborted
│   ├── cancel → markCancelled
│   ├── restart → abort + coordinator.dispatch (new child session)
│   ├── resume → sendPromptAsync (REUSES childSessionId) + new record with resumedFrom
│   ├── chain → sendPromptAsync (REUSES childSessionId) + new record with chainedFrom
│   ├── adjust-prompt → sendPromptAsync to running session
│   └── change-agent → abort + sendPromptAsync with new agent
│
├── recordChildMessageSignal()     ← from chat.message hook
├── recordChildToolSignal()        ← from tool.execute.after hook
└── canSessionAccessDelegation()   ← lineage-based access control
```

**Session hierarchy tracking:**
- 10 slots per parent session (enforced by `SlotManager`)
- Delegation records tracked in in-memory `Map<string, Delegation>` + persisted via `delegation-persistence.ts`
- Parent session ID stored in `Delegation.parentSessionId`; child session ID in `Delegation.childSessionId`
- `resumedFrom`, `chainedFrom`, `restartedFrom` fields on `Delegation` type trace delegation lineage
- Route notifications via `NotificationRouter.register(delegationId, parentSessionId)` — prevents cross-session pollution

---

## 5. TASK vs. DELEGATION DIFFERENTIATION

| Dimension | Native Task Tool | delegate-task Tool |
|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|
| **Mechanism** | OpenCode native subagent dispatch | SDK child-session via `sendPromptAsync` |
| **Monitoring** | None (fire-and-forget) | ✅ Progressive polling 30→45→60→90→120→180s |
| **Failure detection** | None | ✅ 60→120→180→300s action-count checkpoints |
| **Notification** | None | ✅ TUI system_reminder on completion/failure |
| **Control tools** | None | ✅ abort, cancel, restart, resume, chain, adjust-prompt, change-agent |
| **Context preservation** | None (new session each time) | ✅ Session ID tracking, resume/chain reuse same session |
| **Slot management** | None | ✅ 10 slots per parent session |
| **Schema validation** | OpenCode native | Zod schema with `.refine()` for conditional field validation |
| **PTY/Background** | N/A | N/A (covered by CP-PTY phases) |

---

# DEEP SYNTHESIS REPORT: Phases 13, 14, 15 — Session Trackers, Monitor/Notification, and Delegate-Task Gap Remediation

**Synthesized:** 2026-05-22
**Files consumed:** 52 `.md` files across 3 phase directories

---

## 1. Phase-by-Phase Summary Table

| Dimension | Phase 13 | Phase 14 | Phase 15 |
|-----------|----------|----------|----------|
| **Title** | Session Tracker Defect Remediation | Wire Monitor/Notification into DelegationManager | Delegate-Task Gap Remediation (Resume, Delivery, Rich Notifications) |
| **Domain** | Session capture pipeline — hooks → disk | Delegation lifecycle monitoring, failure detection, TUI notifications | Surgical gap fixes for resume, pending replay, rich notifications, control actions, completion detection |
| **Starting State** | Pipeline BROKEN — 4/5 child sessions dual-registered; turnCount always 0; child .json files are skeletons (1 turn); legacy event-tracker double-capturing | Partial edits exist but unverified; escalation-timer design fundamentally wrong (WARN→NUDGE→ALERT→TERMINATE); 65% spec compliance | Phase 14 implemented ~65% spec — 3 critical gaps (C1-C3), 3 medium gaps (M1-M3), 2 minor gaps (N1-N2) |
| **Sub-plans** | 6 (01-06) | 4 (01-04) | 5 (01-05) |
| **Total sub-tasks** | ~18 (2-4 per plan) | ~10 (2-3 per plan) | ~14 (2-3 per plan) |
| **Files modified** | ~16 source + test files | ~15 source + test + artifacts | ~12 source + test files |
| **Tests** | 256 session-tracker tests; 12 integration tests | 271 delegation tests across 11 files; 2384 full-suite tests | 259-282 delegation tests across 15 files |
| **REQs covered** | 13 (REQ-ST-01 through 13) | 8 (P14-R1 through R8) | 6 (REQ-01 through 06) + GAP-N1 |
| **Completion** | ✅ Complete — all 13 REQs | ✅ Complete — all 8 REQs (live UAT still BLOCKED) | ✅ Complete — all 6 REQs, all 8 gaps closed |
| **Methodology** | Disk-truth-first (L1 evidence before any claim) | TDD RED→GREEN; dirty-worktree snapshots; semantic cleanup audit | TDD RED→GREEN; gap-analysis-driven; pure function additions |
| **Architecture impact** | Repaired existing session-tracker pipeline; single-path init; serial write queues | New dispatch path for monitor/notification wiring; control tools suite; slot management | sendPromptAsync injection path; 4-condition completion; pending replay; rich notifications |

---

## 2. CROSS-CUTTING PATTERNS

### 2.1 Methodology Continuity
All three phases share a **TDD RED→GREEN→REFACTOR** pattern with atomic commits per sub-task. Each plan file includes `<must_haves>` with typed truths, `<behavior>` with test specs, and automated verification commands. Phase 13 uniquely uses **disk-truth-first** (L1 evidence validation against `.hivemind/` file content) — this corrected a prior false claim that "12 of 14 defects fixed in code."

### 2.2 Dependency Injection at Composition Root
All three phases reinforce the pattern established earlier: `src/plugin.ts` is the **sole composition root**. Dependencies are constructed in `setupDelegationModules()` and passed as constructor options with `Pick<Interface, "methodName">` typed partials. No singleton globals, no module-level state.

### 2.3 Serial Write Queues
Phase 13 established the `enqueueWrite` + `detectStaleQueue` pattern for concurrent filesystem safety. This was applied to `SessionIndexWriter` (per-session queues) and `ChildWriter` (per-child-file queues). Phase 14 and 15 extend this pattern into the delegation layer but don't need filesystem-level queues (they use in-memory state via lifecycle + continuity).

### 2.4 Hook-Observer Boundary
The CQRS rule (hooks observe, manager/coordinator mutates) is respected across all three phases:
- **Phase 13:** Hooks → `sessionTracker.handleX()` methods → persistence layer (no direct fs writes from hooks)
- **Phase 14:** `chat.message` → `delegationManager.recordChildMessageSignal()`; `tool.execute.after` → `recordChildToolSignal()`
- **Phase 15:** No new hook patterns — all changes are in coordination/manager/plugin layers

### 2.5 WaiterModel Dispatch Preservation
All phases preserve the existing WaiterModel dispatch pattern (async delegation via `sendPromptAsync` → child session runs independently → monitor polls for completion). Phase 15 specifically adds a *second* dispatch path for resume/chain (`sendPromptAsync` to existing child session) that explicitly preserves the WaiterModel — it does NOT create a new session.

---

## 3. NOTIFICATION DELIVERY MECHANISMS — COMPLETE MAP

### 3.1 All Pathways

| # | Mechanism | SDK Method(s) | Where Wired | Used For | Phase |
|---|-----------|---------------|-------------|----------|-------|
| **N1** | TUI append (thin-line) | `client.tui.appendPrompt({ text })` | `plugin.ts` deliver callback; wrapped as `appendTuiPrompt(client, text)` | Live parent session notification — progress, success, failure | 14 |
| **N2** | TUI toast | `client.tui.showToast({ message, variant })` | `plugin.ts:164` — **REMOVED in Phase 15** | Was redundant alongside N1; Phase 15 GAP-N1 removed it | 14 (removed in 15) |
| **N3** | Session prompt (system_reminder blocks) | `client.session.prompt({ noReply: true, parts: [...] })` | `src/task-management/lifecycle/index.ts:replayPendingNotifications()` via `notification-handler.ts` | Full system_reminder delivery when parent session is known to be alive | 14, 15 |
| **N4** | Pending notification queue → continuity | `patchSessionContinuity(sessionID, { pendingNotifications: [...] })` | `plugin.ts:persistPendingDelegationNotifications()` | When parent session ended → notification queued for later replay | 14, 15 |
| **N5** | Init-time drain from continuity | `listSessionContinuity()` → `appendTuiPrompt(client, line)` for each pending | `plugin.ts:replayPendingDelegationNotifications()` (fire-and-forget at plugin init) | Recovers notifications that were queued while session was ended | 15 |
| **N6** | Lifecycle event-triggered replay | `replayPendingNotificationsForEvent()` on `session.created`/`session.updated` | `src/task-management/lifecycle/index.ts:169-192` | Replays via N3 when parent session reconnects | 14 (pre-existing) |
| **N7** | User-direct control action | `sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, { parts: [{ type: "text", text }] })` | `DelegationManager.controlDelegation()` for resume/chain/adjust-prompt | Sends new prompt directly into an existing child session (no monitoring) | 15 |

### 3.2 Notification Delivery Flow (End-to-End)

```
Delegation completes/fails
  → Monitor.onComplete/onFailure fires
    → coordinator.handleCompletion() or manager.handleFailure()
      → notificationRouter.route(notification)
        → route() checks idempotency key (delegationId:type:message)
          → deliver callback (plugin.ts):
            (a) If session alive: N1 (appendTuiPrompt)
            (b) If session ended: N4 (queuePending → persist to continuity)
              → Later, on session resume:
                - N6 fires (lifecycle replay via N3)
                - AND/OR N5 fires (init-time drain via N1)
```

### 3.3 Critical Design Decisions

- **Idempotency:** All terminal notifications carry `idempotencyKey: ${delegationId}:${type}:${message}` to prevent duplicate delivery
- **Double-notification prevention:** Both N5 and N6 read from the same `pendingNotifications[]` array and both `patchSessionContinuity(sessionID, { pendingNotifications: [] })` after successful replay. Whichever runs first clears the array; the other sees empty.
- **`appendTuiPrompt` has NO `sessionID` parameter** in the SDK — this means multi-parent routing cannot be proven at unit-test level. Live UAT is required for L1 evidence.

### 3.4 Code Paths Where `sendPrompt()` / `session.prompt()` Are Used

| File | Line(s) | Pattern | Phase |
|------|---------|---------|-------|
| `src/shared/session-api.ts:183-195` | `sendPromptAsync(client, sessionID, body)` | Wraps `client.session.promptAsync()` | 14, 15 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager-runtime.ts:242` | `sendPromptAsync(this.client, childSessionId, promptBody)` | Initial dispatch → new child session | 14 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/sdk-child-session-starter.ts:36` | `sendPromptAsync(client, childSessionId, {...})` | SDK child session starter | 14 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~296` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Resume/chain — relivers to existing child session | 15 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~268` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Adjust-prompt — supplementary prompt to running session | 15 |
| `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:~284` | `this.options.sendPromptAsync(childSessionId, prompt)` | Change-agent — sends prompt with new agent name | 15 |
| `src/task-management/lifecycle/index.ts:~180` | `replayPendingNotifications(client, sessionID, pending)` | Lifecycle replay via N3 | 14 (pre-existing) |
| `src/coordination/delegation/coordinator.ts:~213` | `sendPromptAsync(previousChildSessionId, step.prompt)` | Chain-append from coordinator | 15 |

| **Session management** | Dual indices, child.json, serial queues | In-memory delegation + continuity persistence | Resume/chain session reuse, pending replay |
| **Notification path** | None (session tracker only) | TUI append + pending queue + lifecycle replay | Init-time drain + rich fields + toast removed |
| **Control surface** | Fixes only (no new tools) | 7 control actions (abort/cancel/restart/resume/chain/adjust-prompt/change-agent) + slot management | Same 7 actions, but resume/chain now truly reuse session ID |
| **Completion** | 3 conditions (stalled + assistant + fileChanges) | Same 3 conditions | 4 conditions (+ totalToolActivityDuration > 60s) |
| **Test approach** | Disk-truth integration tests | TDD RED→GREEN with fake timers | TDD RED→GREEN with mathematical constraint validation |
